title: AI Fluency: Essentials for the Modern Workplace
source: Authored for the Salareen corporate catalog; structured to mirror the UK apprenticeship standard format.

knowledge.K1: What generative AI and large language models are, and that they predict plausible text rather than 'know' facts.
knowledge.K2: Common workplace applications of AI assistants: drafting, summarising, rewriting, analysing and explaining.
knowledge.K3: How a prompt's role, task, context and format shape the quality of AI output.
knowledge.K4: What hallucination is and why AI can state false information confidently.
knowledge.K5: Why AI output must be verified against trusted sources before use.
knowledge.K6: Data-protection principles and the risks of sharing confidential or personal data with AI tools.
knowledge.K7: The difference between approved enterprise AI tools and public consumer tools.
knowledge.K8: Sources of bias in AI systems and their potential impact on people.
knowledge.K9: Principles of responsible and transparent AI use at work.
knowledge.K10: How grounding an assistant in your own documents improves relevance and accuracy.
knowledge.K11: The limits of AI and when a human decision is required.
knowledge.K12: How to build personal habits and reusable prompts for lasting productivity.

skills.S1: Choose appropriate everyday tasks where an AI assistant adds value.
skills.S2: Write clear prompts specifying role, task, context and output format.
skills.S3: Iterate on prompts to refine and improve AI output.
skills.S4: Provide source material to ground an assistant's answers.
skills.S5: Critically review and verify AI output for accuracy and tone.
skills.S6: Identify and avoid sharing sensitive data with unapproved tools.
skills.S7: Apply the organisation's AI and data policy in daily work.
skills.S8: Recognise bias or errors in AI output and respond appropriately.
skills.S9: Be transparent about where AI was used when honesty matters.
skills.S10: Maintain human oversight of decisions affecting people.
skills.S11: Build and reuse a personal library of effective prompts.
skills.S12: Share useful AI techniques to help colleagues improve.

behaviours.B1: Curious and willing to experiment with new AI tools through self-directed learning.
behaviours.B2: Takes personal responsibility for the accuracy and quality of AI-assisted work.
behaviours.B3: Collaborates and shares knowledge openly with colleagues.
behaviours.B4: Acts with integrity and honesty regarding AI use.
behaviours.B5: Protects data and privacy in everyday work.

Duty 1.statement: Use AI assistants responsibly to support everyday work tasks.

Duty 2.statement: Craft and refine effective prompts to get useful results.

Duty 3.statement: Verify and take responsibility for AI-assisted output.

Duty 4.statement: Protect data and follow the organisation's AI policy.

Duty 5.statement: Use AI ethically, transparently and with human oversight.

Duty 6.statement: Build lasting AI productivity habits and help others.
